title: Light German Beef Braised
description: A rich and flavorful German dish featuring beef stock concentrate, perfect for a date night dinner. This halal-friendly recipe is easy to prepare and has a tender, fall-apart texture.
instructions: Heat butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef short ribs until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the caraway seeds and allspice and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red wine to the pot and stir to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits.
Return the beef to the pot and add the beef stock concentrate, salt, and pepper.
Cover the pot and transfer to the oven.
Braise the beef for 2 hours, or until tender.
Strain the sauce and discard the solids.
Whisk in the flour to thicken the sauce.
Stir in the heavy cream and cook for 2 minutes.
Serve the beef short ribs with the sauce spooned over the top and garnished with parsley.
Garnish with additional parsley if desired.
